math_recheck: Re-derived against arXiv 2010.00554 src + appendix: Tr(M) collapse, R-diagonal eigen-identity, unique-strict-Nash simplex-LP (u_i=Σ_{p≥i}Λ_pp z_p), gradient=2M[Oja after generalized Gram-Schmidt], GHA-not-a-gradient (non-symmetric Jacobian) and the projected-first-term equivalence, sinusoid Lemma (period pi), A sin^2-Bsin(2theta)/2+C form, arctan soft-step maximizer cases (A<0/=0/>0 via sign(cos2theta)sign(A)), A<=-g_i+8(i-1)Lambda_11*eps bound, |theta_i*|<=8c_i with c_i<=1/16, boundedness via Euler v^T grad u = 2u_i (paper's Cor B.18 drops the factor 2; deliverable correctly uses 1/2|v^T grad|, final |u_i|<=L_i preserved), sufficient-decrease xi=xi'=(8/5)L_i, c_i recursion and T_k=O(k[(16Lambda_11)^k(k-1)!/Prod g_j /theta_tol]^2) — all confirmed correct; ran the numpy code (both riemannian True/False), recovers all top-k components |cos|=1 with exact eigenvalues.
fixes: context.md:94-98 softened the GAN 'cultural precedent' close — removed the clause prescribing that a fundamental unsupervised task be phrased as several agents each maximizing their own payoff (conceptual leak of the method's central move); kept the legitimate two-player-zero-sum vs many-player-general-sum background.
